items.synopsis_full: Nico, Will and Hazel delve into the mysterious disappearances of monsters at Camp Jupiter. (Ages 10 to 14)

items.title: En Territorio Salvaje
items.author: Erin Hunter
items.synopsis_full: A graphic novel adaptation of "Into the Wild" and "Fire and Ice."

items.title: You With The Sad Eyes
items.author: Christina Applegate
items.synopsis_short: The Emmy Award-winning actress, who received…
items.synopsis_full: The Emmy Award-winning actress, who received a multiple sclerosis diagnosis in 2021, shares personal stories. Read by the author. 8 hours, 27 minutes unabridged.

items.title: Regime Change
items.author: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
items.synopsis_short: Two White House correspondents for The…
items.synopsis_full: Two White House correspondents for The New York Times delve into the first year of President Trump's second term.

items.title: Amanecer de la Cosecha
items.author: Suzanne Collins
items.synopsis_full: The fifth book in the “Hunger Games” series. Read by Jefferson White. 12 hours, 48 minutes unabridged.

items.title: The Pumpkin Queen
items.author: Megan Shepherd
items.synopsis_short: The adventures of Sally, the Pumpkin…
items.synopsis_full: The adventures of Sally, the Pumpkin Queen of Halloween Town. (Ages 12 and up)

items.title: Una trenza de hierba sagrada
items.author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
items.synopsis_short: A botanist and member of the…
items.synopsis_full: A botanist and member of the Citizen Potawatomi Nation espouses having an understanding and appreciation of plants and animals.

items.title: The Anxious Generation
items.author: Jonathan Haidt
items.synopsis_full: A co-author of “The Coddling of the American Mind” looks at the mental health impacts that a phone-based life has on children.

items.title: London Falling
items.author: Patrick Radden Keefe
items.synopsis_full: The author of “Say Nothing” details the efforts by the parents of a 19-year-old Londoner to uncover the truth about his mysterious death and secret life.

items.title: The Warmth Of Other Suns
items.author: Isabel Wilkerson
items.synopsis_short: An account of the Great Migration…
items.synopsis_full: An account of the Great Migration of 1915-70, in which nearly six million African-Americans abandoned the South.
